tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: teammate_A offered help, but I'll decline since they're busy. My plan to finish my share is still solid.

desires: Not look like I'm dragging my feet or being difficult to teammate_A, even though they dumped extra work on me.
Get this done quickly so I can stop thinking about it and move on to something else without feeling overwhelmed.

goals.description: Finish my own project share and politely decline teammate_A's offer to take their part, explaining I need the time for dinner with the cohort.
goals.rationale: I can't let them think I'm dragging my feet or being difficult (avoidant conflict), but I also don't want to do extra work that will make me feel overwhelmed later. Since I value relationships so much, keeping the peace by accepting their help feels safer than pushing back, even though it means less sleep.

goals.description: Eat lunch with cohort_3 immediately after finishing my task.
goals.rationale: Dinner is scheduled for daily, and hanging out with close friends is way more important than grinding on this soft project now. I'll drop the deadline anxiety until they're gone.

agent.last_reasoning: Avoidant conflict: declining feels risky to the relationship, so I'll accept their help while politely setting a boundary about my dinner with cohort_3. This preserves closeness without pushing back hard, keeping peace for now despite the extra stress.
